app.title: Serve Analysis
app.heroSubtitle: Upload a side-view clip of a single serve. Pose is detected on-device, the serve is split into phases, and rule-based checks flag movement errors.

controls.maxLowVisHelp: Higher — try to analyze even noisy clips.
controls.phaseSpeedHelp: Applied when a phase block is selected — click a block to replay it slowly.
controls.phaseHint: Click a phase to replay it slowly. Double-click the video to clear.

skeleton.errorHint: The skeleton is overlaid on the video (preview). Scrub the video to inspect detection: red dots mark joints where MediaPipe is unsure.

errors.video-too-long: Video is longer than 30 seconds. Upload a short clip of a single serve.
errors.serve-not-recognized: Could not recognize the serve. Shoot from the side, with the player fully in frame.
errors.pose-extraction-failed: Could not start pose recognition. Try a different browser/file.
errors.analysis-failed: Internal error while parsing poses. The skeleton is shown for diagnostics.
errors.video-read-failed: Could not read the video. Try a different file.

C3.title: Knee bend
C3.advice: Knees are barely bent — the legs contribute almost no energy to the hit. Bend the knees deeper in the "trophy" position to push upward toward the ball.

detect.too-few-frames: Only {{n}} frame(s) with a full skeleton were extracted — MediaPipe could barely find the figure (player too small/far, poor light, or heavy motion blur).
detect.low-visibility: Key joints are not visible on {{lowPct}}% of {{total}} frames (reject threshold {{maxPct}}%, visibility threshold {{visTh}}). Most often missing: {{worst}}. Usually the cause is framing (legs/knees cut off) or the player is too small.
